items.headline: Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee, March 17–18, 2026
items.whyItMatters: Broad-market potential because it can shift expectations around rates, growth, inflation, or policy.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Crédit Agricole S.A. and Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank, Mega International Commercial Bank Co., Ltd, and the Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
items.whyItMatters: Most relevant to Financials because it may change the near-term outlook for that slice of the market.

items.headline: Trump says Iran 'better stop now' if it's charging oil tankers fees to go through Strait of Hormuz
items.whyItMatters: Energy-supply or pricing signal that can spill into inflation expectations, energy equities, and broad risk sentiment.
items.summary: President Donald Trump said that Iran "better stop now" if it's charging fees to oil tankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Iran attacks on crucial Saudi pipeline and production facilities slash kingdom's oil output
items.whyItMatters: Energy-supply or pricing signal that can spill into inflation expectations, energy equities, and broad risk sentiment.
items.summary: The damage to Saudi infrastructure will only compound the disruption to global oil supplies trigged by Iran's attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Oil rally loses steam after Israel agrees to negotiate with Lebanon
items.whyItMatters: Energy-supply or pricing signal that can spill into inflation expectations, energy equities, and broad risk sentiment.
items.summary: Oil prices were volatile Thursday as the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement remained fragile.

items.headline: SEC Announces Enforcement Results for Fiscal Year 2025
items.whyItMatters: Regulatory/policy signal that could matter beyond one headline, especially for Financials, Industrials, Technology.
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission today announced enforcement results for the fiscal year that ended on September 30, 2025.Central to an effective enforcement program is determining which cases to bring and responsibly stewarding Commission…

items.headline: SEC Appoints David Woodcock as Director of the Division of Enforcement
items.whyItMatters: Regulatory/policy signal that could matter beyond one headline, especially for Financials, Industrials, Technology.
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission today announced that David Woodcock has been appointed Director of the Division of Enforcement, effective May 4, 2026. Mr. Woodcock is currently a partner in the Dallas and Washington, D.C. offices of Gibson, Dunn…
